entry.title: Washington Post: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine losing up to 200 fighters daily; Putin links war to Russia’s imperial past
  • Putin likens himself to Peter the Great, links imperial expansion to Ukraine war
  • Ukraine’s LGBTQ rights movement contends with war’s mixed impact
  • The Dalit filmmaker challenging India’s caste discrimination
  • Biden’s hemispheric summit may end up a dud
  • Paula Rego, whose art captured ‘the beautiful grotesque,’ dies at 87
  • Got burping livestock? In New Zealand, you might be taxed.
  • Three foreign fighters sentenced to death as Russia lashes out
  • Russian forces pummel key cities as they advance in eastern Ukraine
  • Iran will remove 27 cameras from nuclear sites, U.N. watchdog says
  • How a sleeping 2-year-old and his dad won the Buffalo Marathon
  • Berlin car-ramming suspect shows signs of mental illness, police say
  • Separatist court gives death sentences to Britons, Moroccan who fought for Ukraine
  • Thailand legalizes marijuana — with gray areas and caveats
  • Belgian king offers ‘regrets’ but no apology for colonialism in Congo
  • A deadly disease is wiping out Caribbean coral, researchers find
  • WHO covid origins report says ‘lab leak’ theory needs further investigation
  • Microplastics found in fresh Antarctic snow, as pollutants spread
  • Pakistan probes Hindu temple's desecration as India protests
  • Pope cancels trip to Congo and South Sudan due to knee issue
  • Asylum-seekers make UK legal bid to stop Rwanda deportations
  • Syria suspends Damascus airport flights after Israel strike
  • Toes-for-cash hoax reflects Zimbabwe fears of soaring prices
  • German priest found dead after suspension over abuse probe
  • Greece: Cocaine found in banana shipment, 4 Britons arrested
  • A baby vanished after her parents’ murder in the ’80s. She’s been found.
  • Ohio officer who ran over shooting victim won’t be charged in his death
  • New video released in Jan. 6 hearing shows mob gathering, violence
  • She got an STD during car sex. Now, Geico could pay her $5.2 million.
  • Post Politics Now: After dramatic first night, Jan. 6 panel plans several more high-profile hearings
  • Capitol Police officer Caroline Edwards recounts Jan. 6 ‘war scene’
  • The Jan. 6 hearing dominated the airwaves — except on Fox News
  • Jan. 6 committee uses video, testimony to tell tale of the insurrection
  • Jan. 6 hearing recap: Police officer describes bloody ‘war scene’ outside Capitol
  • Jan. 6 panel to hear from Capitol Police officer, filmmaker as high-drama hearings begin
  • Mich. gubernatorial candidate arrested on Jan. 6 Capitol riot charge
  • D.C. chiropractor being sued in officer’s assault is charged in Jan. 6 riot
  • FBI says he ran a crypto Ponzi scheme. Investors refuse to believe it.
  • Gas prices keep inflation at highest level in 40 years in May
  • Jewelry giant settles gender-discrimination lawsuit for $175 million
  • Mortgage rates move higher ahead of Federal Reserve meeting
  • What Europe’s push to simplify chargers means for you
  • Modern house on Massachusetts Avenue offered at $5 million
  • Maxed-out townhouse sets the stage for minimal impact on environment
  • Finding the Amazon of Used Cars Looks Like a Pipe Dream
  • US Taxpayers Got Little From a College’s Canceled Debt
  • Iran Has Overplayed Its Hand in Nuclear Talks
  • What Will Postwar Ukraine Look Like? Planning Starts Before Peace
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • Insurrection? Sedition? Incitement? A Legal Guide to the Capitol Riot
  • How Putin’s Spooking Japan Further Away From Pacifism
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • Liz Cheney got her chance to rebut her dishonorable peers. She didn't miss.
  • Why the Jan. 6 hearings’ vital task is to show Americans what happened
  • The Jan. 6 committee may have bruised Trump, but he’s still standing
  • Americans deserve to know if ATF will continue ‘stash house stings’
  • 6 takeaways from the Jan. 6 committee’s powerful case against Trump
  • Taylor Lorenz said an editor was to blame. Is that okay?
  • Trump has secured his place in history
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • No signs of Russian threat to Sweden, Finland: NATO deputy chief
  • Singapore and South Korea sign 2 MOUs on defence, media cooperation
  • Analysis-Buy Now Pay Later business model faces test as rates rise

  • Capacity limits, COVID-19 tests no longer needed at nightlife establishments from Jun 14

  • Former tour guide Yang Yin, who cheated elderly widow of S$1.1m, deported after serving jail term
  • 277 suspects investigated for scams where victims lost more than S$5.8 million
  • US Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin and Ng Eng Hen affirm need for stable defence relations
  • Heartbroken to see her kids’ bloody scratches from eczema, this mum started a business to help them

  • Froome says no guarantees he'll be selected for Tour de France

  • Poulter to appeal PGA Tour suspension over LIV Golf involvement
  • South Africa's Miller carries red-hot IPL form into India T20 series

  • Ukraine says troops holding on to Sievierodonetsk, advance in south

entry.title: Guardian: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v New Zealand: second Test, day one – live!
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Kremlin defends death sentences for captured Britons; UK MP says pair being ‘used as hostages’ – live
  • Peter Blake illustrates Under Milk Wood and Peter Saul cartoonish compositions come to London – the week in art
  • Digested week: marmalade sandwiches, cross princes and counting medals | Emma Brockes
  • Arsenal win race to sign Brazilian teenager Marquinhos in £3m deal
  • Factory farming is turning this beautiful British river into an open sewer | George Monbiot
  • Pitch perfect: how women’s football revolutionised the game – and changed my life

  • Julee Cruise, singer and frequent David Lynch collaborator, dies aged 65
  • Emma Raducanu must focus on long-term pursuit of glory as injuries take toll | Tumaini Carayol

  • January 6 hearing: Trump was at heart of plot that led to ‘attempted coup’
  • We’re almost out of ammunition and relying on western arms, says Ukraine

  • ‘Ironclad brothers’: what China wants from its role in Cambodia’s biggest naval base
  • Nasa forms independent team to study unexplained UFO sightings
  • I’m wondering if my mother has dyslexia. Should I tell her or let things be? | Leading questions
  • Nations League: Kosovo defeat deepens Northern Ireland woes, Spain edge Swiss
  • UK healthcare staff call in sick to avoid using car as cost of fuel soars, union says
  • LIV Series offers no sign of revolution against backdrop of golf’s civil war | Ewan Murray

  • Most senior Uefa security officials ‘not actively involved’ in Champions League final control room

  • Pressure mounts on Senate to act on gun safety amid Republican resistance

entry.title: NY Times: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 10 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-10 11:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong court tells former ESF worker accused of taking bribes for kindergarten placements to surrender travel documents
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong police arrest three more people for submitting fake Covid-19 results to authorities as city logs 672 new cases
  • Hong Kong’s New World Development sells US$700 million dual-tranche green, social bond in global first
  • Opposition veteran Yeung Sum expresses no regret over loss of Hong Kong government honours alongside two peers
  • Research co-led by CityU boosts efficiency of perovskite solar cells to record high
  • Can Hong Kong become a ‘legal hub’? State official urges city to lean on strengths to aid country’s ‘foreign-related’ work in field, such as in treaties and international legislation
  • Implementing core values from the start at YCIS
  • Law stopping Hong Kong bosses from raiding MPF funds to cover staff payment obligations is too little, too late, critics argue
  • Two shots fired in gang clash near Hong Kong’s Lan Kwai Fong nightlife hub, as knife-wielding attackers ambush group
  • Why Hong Kong has changed its Covid-19 case reporting criteria, and how this affects you
  • Jumbo Floating Restaurant saga another sign of Hong Kong’s inability to preserve its contemporary history
  • Former employee of scandal-hit Hong Kong child protection group pleads guilty to abuse, first of 27 suspects to do so
  • Hong Kong bosses should be flexible with workers caring for elderly or disabled residents at home, study says
  • Owner of domestic helper agency in Hong Kong denies wrongdoing over death of worker
  • Hong Kong heritage advisers urge officials to work with advocates to reassess value of pre-war building once used for spying against Japanese during World War II
  • 8 Labrador puppies bred to detect drugs, explosives under programme between customs, Hong Kong airport’s security company
  • New training school for Hong Kong government workers to focus on raising awareness of national security, college head says
  • As China’s capital reopens restaurants, foodies and lunch crowd remain reluctant to dine out
  • Tencent rolls out simplified employee rating system amid job cuts, other cost-saving measures
  • China, US defence chiefs square off on Taiwan in first face-to-face talks
  • China’s new bank loans nearly triple in May as Beijing steps up policy support
  • Shanghai warns public to avoid crowds as it prepares to test 14 million residents for coronavirus on Saturday
  • ‘Angel particle’ mimics may be first step to reliable quantum computers
  • China’s gaokao university entrance exam: family celebrates end of daughter’s test with banner declaring she can now ‘fall in love’
  • Climate change: why multinational firms, eager to buy green power in China, find it hard to get enough to meet their decarbonisation goals
  • Tencent pulls plug on Twitch-like platform Penguin Esports as regulators freeze out the social media giant from new gaming licences
  • China diplomat outlines complicated causes of Sri Lanka’s debt crisis
  • Chinese help for Cambodian naval base does not mean PLA troops on the ground, say analysts
  • China inflation: increased demand as coronavirus restrictions ease could push up prices
  • Earth’s inner secrets revealed in oldest rocks found on ocean floor
  • US is so bent on resisting China’s rise, it has ignored its own decline
  • Chinese game developer denies ‘pay to work’ offer after job post sparks controversy amid tough job market
  • Coronavirus: China moves to calm foreign investors after ‘unprecedented’ shock of Shanghai lockdown
  • US is facing supply chain challenges, but so is China, experts say
  • US taking aim at industrial labour shortfall and China supply dependency, Pentagon official says
  • How much is China’s foreign direct investment and is it still a good destination for overseas investors?
  • In the eyes of others, the US is not the benign power it thinks it is
  • WHO says Covid-19 origins unclear but lab leak theory needs study
  • China-Germany economic ties shift with Xinjiang allegations, zero Covid and supply chain issues
  • Inflation isn’t China’s problem, income is: ‘all we can do is spend less’
  • China’s chip import volume declines in first five months as rigid Covid-19 control measures disrupt manufacturing sector
  • Beijing hits out at Washington over proposed US$120 million arms deal to Taiwan
  • Bon Odori festival saga sheds light on how Malaysia governs Islam, after sultan rebukes federal minister
  • Singapore’s Changi airport to reopen terminals as travel recovers faster than expected
  • Waste of energy: why Japan’s gamble on power since Fukushima could mean a hot summer, rolling blackouts, and the restart of nuclear plants
  • First in Asia, Thailand makes marijuana legal, but smoking discouraged
  • Malaysia to scrap mandatory death penalty, giving judges sentencing discretion
  • Coronavirus: Thailand ready to scrap last of pandemic-era curbs on tourists
  • Johnny Depp’s ‘US$62,000 bill’ at UK Indian restaurant after winning Amber Heard defamation case spotlights Hollywood’s curry mania
  • IPL: Amazon’s Jeff Bezos, India’s Mukesh Ambani, Disney and Sony to battle over US$7.7 billion rights to Super Bowl of cricket
  • Malaysia Airlines flight MH17: Defence says missile theory unproven as trial wraps
  • Collective effort needed to rebuff China’s advances in Pacific, says New Zealand’s Jacinda Ardern
  • Amid violent unrest, Myanmar’s military junta hopes to revive tourism
  • Japan’s Kishida to send China a warning on Taiwan at Singapore’s Shangri-La Dialogue, analysts say
  • Why won’t Asean condemn Myanmar junta’s plan to hang four people? In case they need to use the same option
  • Stinking piles of rubbish are damaging tourism and locals are ‘forced to live like pigs’ in Nepal’s capital city
  • TikTok adds ‘screen time breaks’ for addicts unable to stop scrolling
  • ‘War scene’: five key moments from the US Capitol riot hearing
  • Ukraine now dependent on arms from allies after exhausting Soviet-era weaponry
  • Biden and Brazil’s Bolsonaro hold first meeting: some nice words, but distant
  • Teslas with Autopilot are closer to recall amid elevated investigation into collisions in US
  • US Chamber of Commerce summit gift bag has ‘Made in China’ goodies
  • US Capitol riot hearing shows Trump allies, daughter Ivanka rejected election fraud claims
  • Three killed, officer wounded in Maryland shooting
  • Now there’s a Sriracha shortage, and spice lovers are up in arms
  • Nasa joins hunt for UFOs, hiring experts to solve ‘perplexing mysteries’
  • Russia making more money off oil and gas than before Ukraine war, US official says
  • Slain Texas couple’s missing baby girl found in Oklahoma 42 years later
  • Vladimir Putin compares Ukraine actions with Peter the Great’s conquests
  • Covid during pregnancy doubled babies’ risk of delayed development in speech, motor skills
  • Convicted R&B singer R Kelly should get more than 25 years in prison, say prosecutors
  • Three foreigners who fought for Ukraine sentenced to death by firing squad
  • Former CSRC official Feng Henian under investigation as Beijing’s anti-corruption crackdown continues
  • Huobi Tech to expand virtual asset ecosystem on back of strong results
  • Why global economies should expect to see out 2022 in better shape than now
  • TSMC’s US$100 billion wipeout does not change analysts’ bullish outlook for Taiwan’s semiconductor giant
  • Hong Kong stocks trim losses, Alibaba rebounds as China inflation report spurs bets for more monetary easing
  • Delisting risk, souring relations are hurdles for Chinese biotech firms seeking to enter US market, analyst says
  • In solving real-world needs, decentralised finance can only continue to grow
  • Electric cars: Nio warns of fewer deliveries this quarter as anti-pandemic curbs hit production, weaken sales
  • Ant Group, China securities regulator deny reports Alibaba’s fintech giant is reviving stalled IPO
  • Genshin Impact developer miHoYo gets a boost as Beijing grants game licence for expected shooting game
  • BTS launch Proof, a triple album of new and old songs, with promise that the best is ‘Yet to Come’
  • With Dior collaboration and exposure from A$AP Rocky, Erl – fashion label that’s a 2022 LVMH Prize winner – is on the up. Its founder takes stock
  • Jhumpa Lahiri, American writer, on her ‘Italian years’ and the freedom adopting another language has given her
  • Happy Hour movie review: Drive My Car director Ryusuke Hamaguchi’s 2015 masterpiece is a five-hour meditation on friendship, marriage and connection
  • Can German food find love in Hong Kong? Restaurants offer modern takes on traditional dishes – it’s not all sausages and sauerkraut
  • Netflix K-drama Welcome to Wedding Hell lays bare the troubles in 2 becoming 1
  • Who is Julia Garner, actress picked to play Madonna in biopic? Her starring roles in Ozark and Inventing Anna on Netflix
  • How to make veal tartare with pickled shallot and Parmesan-paprika palmiers – a fancy dinner party starter that’s lean, light and elegant
  • Who is Rebel Wilson’s new girlfriend, Ramona Agruma? The Pitch Perfect actress came out as LGBT during Pride Month, and she’s dating a fashion designer and owner of sustainable brand Lemon Ve Limon
  • Who is Jason Alexander, Britney Spears’ wedding crasher? Her first husband was arrested at her secret ceremony to Sam Asghari – and is a Trump supporter spotted at the US Capitol riot
  • The 10 best bars in Hong Kong: from Four Seasons’ Argo, The Mandarin Oriental’s The Aubrey and cocktail hotspot Penicillin, to OG molecular mixologist Quinary, Rosewood’s DarkSide and speakeasy 001
  • Is Aespa the new Blackpink? The K-pop supergroups, compared – from Karina, Giselle, Winter and Ningning’s Coachella performances and endorsements with luxury brands like Givenchy, to smash hit debuts
  • Whatever happened to Kate Bush, singer of Stranger Things hit song Running Up That Hill? From her US$60 million net worth and reclusive lifestyle, to her 1985 hit making a chart comeback
  • Inside Johnny Depp’s OTT US$100 million property portfolio: from the Bahamas island where he married Amber Heard, to a French village he bought Vanessa Paradis and a Kentucky farm named after his mum
  • Who is Harry Styles’ girlfriend Olivia Wilde, really? The Tron actress based her stage name on Oscar Wilde, has two kids with Ted Lasso star Jason Sudeikis, and was once even married to a prince
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 10 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • January 6 riot was part of ‘attempted coup’ by Trump, committee alleges
  • Liz Cheney: a Republican profile in courage after the Capitol attack
  • The billion pound battle for prestigious Claridge’s of London
  • Military briefing: Russia’s barrage hits Ukrainian morale in the Donbas

  • Russian-backed court sentences UK and Moroccan ‘mercenaries’ to death

  • Former Nato chief calls for economic version of Article 5 defence pledge

  • To investors seeking income — there’s still plenty of it about
  • ECB to raise interest rates for first time since 2011
  • News updates from the January 6 hearing: Senior DoJ leaders threatened mass resignation to prevent Trump overturning election, Cheney’s opening presentations stand out and Proud Boys and Oath Keepers play central role
  • Iran to remove 27 cameras from nuclear facilities
  • Asian demographics/baby formula: US demand will be a shortlived boost to industry hit by low birth rates
  • European gas prices surge after fire at Texas LNG plant
  • A tangle of cables is the price for Apple’s innovation
  • Big Tech tiptoes to the metaverse
  • Palantir seeks blockbuster NHS deal
  • The global race for supercomputing power
  • Ordinary Ukrainians wage war with digital tools and drones
  • The Lex Newsletter: chaotic drift at Credit Suisse — and in world affairs
  • The last Prius to freedom, or why advance planning is my key to happiness
  • ‘I cannot wait to see bison roaming through Kent woodland’
  • The world must brace itself for a further surge in oil prices
  • Learning how to think well involves hearts as well as minds
  • The UK’s credit gap will gape in this cost of living crisis
  • The LME debacle raises serious questions for the City of London
  • Food stocks: price rises keep inflationary pressure at bay, for now

  • Britain under Johnson has abandoned ideals of political leadership
  • Helping Moldova is in Europe’s interest
  • In praise of the ‘holiday read’
  • Boots/Reliance: Ambani could reinvigorate UK chemist chain

  • VOX POPULI: Working solo at an eatery is like climbing a mountain alone
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
